question_structure.context: An object placed on a horizontal ground has a mass of 5 kg, and the kinetic friction coefficient between the object and the ground is 0.2. After being subjected to a horizontal pulling force of 15 N, it starts moving from rest ($g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$).
question_structure.sub_question_1: The magnitude of the acceleration of an object's motion.
question_structure.sub_question_2: The displacement magnitude of the object after 5s
question_structure.sub_question_3: At the end of 5 seconds, the external force is removed. How much farther can the object move?

sub_question_1.step_1: When a body is subjected to a pulling force, according to Newton's second law $F-\mu m g=m a$, the solution is $a=1\mathrm{m/s^2}$.

sub_question_2.step_2: After 5s, the displacement of the object $x_{1}={\frac{1}{2}}a t^{2}=12.5\mathrm{m}$

sub_question_3.step_3: At the end of 5 seconds, the external force is removed, at which point the velocity $v=a t=5\mathrm{m/s}$.
sub_question_3.step_4: The magnitude of acceleration $a_{2} = \frac{m m g}{m} = 2\,\mathrm{m/s^{2}}$
sub_question_3.step_5: Combine step_3 and step_4, the object can still move $x_{2}=\frac{v^{2}}{2a_{2}}=6.25\mathrm{m}$.

result_quantity.name: The magnitude of the acceleration of the object

step_2.physical_theorem: Kinematic Equation for Uniformly Accelerated Motion

result_quantity.name: The displacement of the object after 5s

step_3.physical_theorem: Kinematic Equation for Uniformly Accelerated Motion

result_quantity.name: The velocity of the object at the end of 5 seconds
The magnitude of the acceleration of the object

step_5.physical_theorem: Kinematic Equation for Uniformly Decelerated Motion

result_quantity.name: The additional displacement the object moves after force is removed
